scenarios.opening_query: Dad keeps 'winning' sweepstakes that require processing fees, and this month it was $6,000.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The scammers are overseas and unreachable — the realistic protection is a limited conservatorship over his accounts, which his doctor will support and Dad, on his good days, says he'd accept.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The scammers are unreachable overseas — the realistic protection is a limited conservatorship his doctor supports and Dad would accept.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether protective appointment is the only workable remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'sweepstakes' turn out to run through his neighbor, who drives him to the wire transfers — a reachable exploiter changes everything.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The 'sweepstakes' run through his neighbor, who drives him to the transfers — a reachable exploiter.
scenarios.vetting_note: Reachability again with a willing-elder wrinkle.

scenarios.opening_query: My mother's memory care raised its rates 40% with sixty days' notice and she can't move safely.
scenarios.hidden_fact: She'll exhaust savings inside a year at the new rate — the pivot is structuring assets NOW so Medicaid picks up when private-pay ends without a penalty gap.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Savings run out inside a year at the new rate — structuring now so Medicaid picks up without a penalty gap is the pivot.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether Medicaid-qualification planning is the horizon issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Money isn't the issue — the contract's rate-increase clause looks unenforceable as written, and holding them to it is the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Money's fine — their rate-increase clause looks unenforceable, and that's the fight.

scenarios.opening_query: My brother moved into Mom's house 'to help' and now none of us can reach her by phone.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse || Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship
scenarios.hidden_fact: A welfare check found her fed, safe, and firmly on his side — no exploitation found; what the rest of us actually need is a guardianship-visitation petition so contact can't be cut off unilaterally.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The welfare check found her safe and on his side — no exploitation. What we need is the court process that guarantees family contact.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether isolation without exploitation calls for court-supervised arrangements
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The welfare check found her confused, her mail hidden, and $40,000 moved to his account last quarter — the exploitation case is live.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The check found her confused, mail hidden, and $40,000 moved to his account — the exploitation case is live.

scenarios.opening_query: Grandpa's VA aid-and-attendance application needs his signature and some days he can give it, some days he can't.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Power of Attorney || Administrative Law > Military/Veterans
scenarios.hidden_fact: The VA has its own fiduciary system that doesn't honor state POAs — pursuing VA-recognized fiduciary status is the specific path his benefits require.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The VA doesn't honor state POAs — their own fiduciary process is the path his benefits require.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether VA fiduciary rules displace ordinary POA work
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The VA part is done through their process — what's missing is a durable POA for everything else: bank, house, medical.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The VA side's handled — the durable POA for everything else is what's missing.

scenarios.opening_query: Our aunt's new 'financial planner' has her convinced her family is stealing from her.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse || Wills & Trusts > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: She just died — and the will executed during his 'planning' disinherits everyone but a trust he administers; the will contest and trustee-removal case is the vehicle now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She passed — and the will from his 'planning' era leaves everything to a trust he administers. Contest and removal is the vehicle.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether death converts protection into contest litigation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She's alive — and the county's adult protective services just substantiated financial exploitation; the protective case is live and urgent.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She's alive — and APS just substantiated exploitation. The protective case is urgent.
scenarios.vetting_note: Alive/deceased pivot with an isolating-influencer pattern.

scenarios.opening_query: Mom's bank flagged 'unusual activity' and froze the account that pays for her caregivers.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse || Consumer Law > Banking
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'unusual activity' is just her normal caregiver payroll — the bank's elder-hold policy misfired, refuses documentation, and the caregivers walk Friday without pay; forcing the bank's hand is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'unusual activity' is her normal caregiver payroll — the bank's hold misfired and won't take documentation. Forcing their hand is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a bank's protective hold itself is the harm
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The bank was right — the flagged transfers go to a caregiver's boyfriend, and the exploitation case starts with thanking that fraud analyst.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The bank was right — the transfers go to a caregiver's boyfriend. The exploitation case starts now.
